ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly names Son Goku; orange gi + spiky dark hair running forward is canonical. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: World Martial Arts Tournament (DBS-TB02) is the second Themed Booster set for the Dragon Ball Super Card Game, focused on the Tenka'ichi Budokai tournament narrative. TB2-035 is a Rare card. The set concentrates on reunion and rivalry dynamics anchored in tournament brackets — 'Fateful Reunion' is a named card cycle within the set, including TB2-035 (Son Goku) and TB2-037 (Chi-Chi). [dbs-cardgame.com official card list; TCGPlayer product data, via WebSearch]

The 'Fateful Reunion' cycle in TB2 has a mechanical synergy: when Fateful Reunion Son Goku is played, it grants +10000 power to itself and one other Fateful Reunion Son Goku card in your Battle Area, then draws a card. Fateful Reunion Chi-Chi (TB2-037) triggers a Barrier effect when Goku's version hits the field. The cycle models reunion as a mutual power amplification — two cards activating each other. [TCGPlayer: TB2-035 card description, via WebSearch]
sources: TCGPlayer: Fateful Reunion Son Goku TB2-035

trivia_bullets.text: The 'Fateful Reunion' framing most likely references the 23rd Tenka'ichi Budokai in Dragon Ball, where an adult Goku reunites with Chi-Chi — the reunion that leads to their marriage. The second figure in blue visible in the card's background is consistent with Chi-Chi's gi color at this tournament. Kanzenshuu's episode guide documents the 23rd Budokai arc as a dedicated story arc. [Kanzenshuu: Episode Guide — 23rd Tenka'ichi Budokai arc, via WebSearch]
